acceptance.criterion: With `B0`/`B1` report ids and zero-based `batch_complete` events, each batch's event signal merges under its own report label, no fabricated multi-source corroboration appears, and same-batch conflict detection can fire; a regression test proves it and fails against the v2.10.1 logic

One shared confidence-projection helper backs all former `_confidence_candidate`- pattern copies in `full_run.py`, with no behavioral divergence left between routes (existing suite green proves preservation)
A validated report row carrying `unsure_about_count` with no `unsure_about` list is rejected or ignored outside the shared-OAuth projection route, with a test covering the `9999`-style abuse case
Prompt-block overflow keeps the highest-attention rows that fit and states how many rows were dropped; reservation truncation states the hidden-item count; tests cover both
Identical reservation sets in different order no longer flag `CONFLICTING SOURCES`; test proves order-insensitivity
Full unittest suite passes with no existing test weakened, skipped, or deleted
Abbreviated long options are rejected: `--effor low` (and `--eff low`) exit with a parse error instead of silently parsing; a regression test proves it

With no explicit `--effort`, the CLI passes `effort=None` and `prepare_full_run`'s normalized resolution is the only default authority: `--adapter Grok-Build` (any case) resolves to the grok default effort; test proves the former normalization mismatch is gone
A prepare with explicit `--effort medium` followed by a `--resume` without `--effort` rebuilds at `medium`, not the flipped default; test covers it
Full unittest suite passes with no existing test weakened, skipped, or deleted
An events file whose final line is torn no longer discards earlier valid events on the reconstruction path: the review context carries those signals; a regression test proves it
When event evidence was discarded or partial, the review block says so instead of claiming no signal was reported; test asserts the wording branch

A test with mocked `reconcile_full_run_with_git` proves `monitor_full_run` / `await_full_run` deliver `review_context` end-to-end, and every CLI print site emitting `review_prompt_block` has at least one test
Full unittest suite passes with no existing test weakened, skipped, or deleted
A one-sided bump of either the pinned Grok upstream short SHA or `GROK_UPSTREAM_SEMANTIC_COMMIT` now fails the consistency policy; tests cover agreeing and disagreeing states
The public-wording guard flags persona claims such as "powered by Fable" while passing `Fable 5` / `claude-fable` model identifiers; tests cover both directions
`references/grok-implementer-launch-prompt.md` no longer contradicts itself on high effort: the anti-pattern row is reframed as an accepted tradeoff of the default
`references/prewalk.md`'s canonical launch example marks execution effort route-dependent instead of pinning `medium`
An operator-visible note records that pre-upgrade Grok qualification canaries recorded at `medium` execution effort must be re-recorded at `high`
`python3 scripts/check_repo_consistency.py` and the full unittest suite pass after the doc edits

`leases.run_git` enforces an explicit default timeout, and the `git_contract.py` git operations (which route through it or bound their own subprocess calls) no longer rely on any unbounded default; tests cover the default and a TimeoutExpired surface

`load_prewalk_capability_evidence` reads through the fd-bound pattern and rejects symlinked/irregular artifacts, sharing one consolidated bounded-reader helper with the existing loaders; tests prove symlink rejection
TODO.md Live no longer lists the two completed items
Full unittest suite passes with no existing test weakened, skipped, or deleted
`CHANGELOG.md` carries a promoted v2.10.2 entry covering the run's fixes including the canary re-record note; `SKILL.md` and `AGENTS.md` frontmatter read `2.10.2`
`python3 scripts/release_checklist.py --version 2.10.2` passes
Issue items 17–20 are recorded as deferred in TODO.md with rationale
`python3 scripts/check_repo_consistency.py` and the full unittest suite pass at the release tip

continuation_guard.next_required_action: Finish staging: sync/validate session, commit+push staging docs, open PR, mint b0 rollback ref, write packet capsule at staged HEAD, launch claude-fable-5/low native worker, park
continuation_guard.reason: Six worker batches, terminal review, and authorized landing ceremony outstanding

handoff.next_exact_action: Begin B1-A1: route report/event batch identity through the canonical normalize_batch_id parser in build_worker_confidence_review_context, honoring parsed 0 and keying the event-report merge on normalized batch number.

master_acceptance.criterion: Issue #86 items 1–16 are each addressed with a regression test or explicit recorded evidence, and the PR description maps every issue item (1–20) to its resolution or deferral
The full unittest suite, `scripts/release_checklist.py --version 2.10.2`, and `scripts/check_repo_consistency.py` are green at the final reviewed tip
Issue items 17–20 are recorded as deferred in TODO.md with rationale, and nothing in the diff starts the deferred decomposition or roadmap features
